description:
Trigger: Linux host with no Secret Service daemon running, then call get() or put().
Expected (per README and module docs): SecretError::Unavailable.
Actual: SecretError::Backend.

Root cause: keyring 3.6 secret_service::decode_error() maps only Locked, NoResult, and Prompt to NoStorageAccess. D-Bus connection failures (no session bus, no daemon name owner) are PlatformFailure. secretx-desktop matches only NoStorageAccess to Unavailable; PlatformFailure falls through to the catch-all Backend arm.

Impact: Callers retry-loop on Unavailable per the SecretError docs; daemon-absent hosts will not retry, contradicting the documented behavior. Worse, a transient D-Bus restart will surface as a permanent Backend error.

Fix options:
1. Map PlatformFailure to Unavailable on Linux (target_os = linux).
2. Inspect the boxed source for connection-refused/NameHasNoOwner heuristics — fragile.
3. Update README and module doc to say Backend instead of Unavailable. Less useful, but truthful.

References: keyring-3.6.3/src/secret_service.rs:580-600, keyring-3.6.3/src/error.rs:24-40.
title: Linux daemon-absent path returns SecretError::Backend, not Unavailable as documented
